claim: Server-Sent Events (SSE) couples response stream health to client connection health; when connection drops, stream is lost and in-progress LLM generation becomes orphaned.
actionable: Decouple agent layer from client layer using durable sessions to persist events independently of any single connection.
source_title: Why Your AI UX Is Broken (and It's Not the Model's Fault) — Mike Christensen, Ably

claim: SSE creates a fundamental conflict between resumability and live control: abort and resume are mutually exclusive because the only signal a client can send over a one-way pipe is closing the connection.
actionable: Replace SSE with bidirectional transports like WebSockets to enable both cancellation (interrupt) and resumability (reconnect) on the same stream.
source_title: Why Your AI UX Is Broken (and It's Not the Model's Fault) — Mike Christensen, Ably

claim: Durable sessions—a shared stateful resource between agent and client layers—enable automatic resumability across disconnects, multi-device sync, and concurrent multi-agent activity without routing all updates through a single orchestrator.
actionable: Implement durable sessions as a pub/sub channel layer (e.g., via Ably channels or similar) so agents write independently and clients subscribe to the session once to see all activity.
source_title: Why Your AI UX Is Broken (and It's Not the Model's Fault) — Mike Christensen, Ably

claim: Three foundational capabilities for great AI products: resilient delivery (survive disconnections), continuity across surfaces (multi-device sync), and live control (steer agent mid-task).
actionable: When designing AI chat UX, audit your implementation against these three: can users reconnect mid-stream, access the session from other devices, and send follow-up messages while the agent is working?
source_title: Why Your AI UX Is Broken (and It's Not the Model's Fault) — Mike Christensen, Ably

claim: Pub/sub channels with properties like independent addressability, message persistence, and automatic resumability form the architectural foundation for durable sessions in AI products.
actionable: Use a pub/sub platform (Ably, Firebase, Redis Streams) as the session layer: clients and agents independently connect to the same channel, messages outlive connections, and reconnects auto-resume from last seen event.
source_title: Why Your AI UX Is Broken (and It's Not the Model's Fault) — Mike Christensen, Ably
